segments.text: It is good to give thanks to the Lord, And to sing praises to Your name, O Most High
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: To declare Your lovingkindness in the morning, And Your faithfulness every night
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: On an instrument of ten strings, On the lute, And on the harp, With harmonious sound
For You, Lord, have made me glad through Your work; I will triumph in the works of Your hands
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: O Lord, how great are Your works! Your thoughts are very deep
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: A senseless man does not know, Nor does a fool understand this
When the wicked spring up like grass, And when all the workers of iniquity flourish, It is that they may be destroyed forever
But You, Lord, are on high forevermore
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For behold, Your enemies, O Lord, For behold, Your enemies shall perish; All the workers of iniquity shall be scattered
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But my horn You have exalted like a wild ox; I have been anointed with fresh oil
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: My eye also has seen my desire on my enemies; My ears hear my desire on the wicked Who rise up against me
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree, He shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon
Those who are planted in the house of the Lord Shall flourish in the courts of our God
They shall still bear fruit in old age; They shall be fresh and flourishing
To declare that the Lord is upright; He is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in Him
segments.speaker: Psalmist
